headlines.headline: Philippines Hit by 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake, Tsunami Warnings Issued
headlines.summary: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao in the southern Philippines, triggering tsunami warnings for the Philippines, Indonesia, Taiwan, and other Pacific islands. The German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) reported the quake struck at a shallow depth.

headlines.headline: Japan Economy Loses Momentum in Q1 as Capital Expenditure Slumps
headlines.summary: Japan's economy lost momentum in the January-March quarter as sluggish capital expenditure pointed to challenges ahead from the Middle East conflict. Revised GDP data showed annualized growth of 1.8%, below initial estimates, as the Iran war impact weighs on the export-dependent economy.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Jump More Than $2 on Israel Strikes in Lebanon
headlines.summary: Oil prices surged more than $2 per barrel after Israel struck Beirut, with US crude futures hitting $93.11. The escalation threatens the fragile ceasefire between Iran and the US, keeping markets on edge amid ongoing Hormuz Strait disruptions.

headlines.headline: Trump Urges Restraint After Iran Fires Missiles at Israel
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump urged restraint after Iran launched missiles at Israel in the first such attack since the ceasefire began. The escalation follows Israeli strikes on Beirut that killed at least two people, with the US pushing to preserve the fragile peace talks.

headlines.headline: Iran threatens US targets after Lebanon escalation
headlines.summary: Iran's top negotiator warned that US bases and Israeli assets in the Middle East are legitimate targets following a US naval blockade of Iran and Israel's escalation in Lebanon.

headlines.headline: Peru presidential runoff too close to call
headlines.summary: Exit polls show Peru's presidential election runoff in a statistical tie as voters choose between competing visions for the country's future amid political turmoil.

perspectives.angle: Peru Votes in Tight Presidential Runoff Testing Latin America's Rightward Shift
perspectives.summary: Peruvians cast ballots in a close presidential runoff between pro-US conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez, a race that could determine whether Latin America's rightward trend continues or reverses.

headlines.headline: US troops and families adjust to new normal of Iran war
headlines.summary: The prolonged state of readiness in the US-Iran conflict is causing significant stress for service members and their families as the war reaches its 100th day.

perspectives.angle: US troops and families adjust to prolonged state of conflict with Iran
perspectives.summary: American military personnel and their families are adapting to an unusual state of conflict with Iran that is neither full-scale war nor peace, with ongoing tensions causing stress for service members stationed in the region.

headlines.headline: Gulf markets fall amid fresh US-Iran escalation
headlines.summary: Most Gulf stock markets closed lower on Sunday after new escalations clouded efforts to end the war between the US and Iran, adding to regional economic uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup team arrives in Mexico amid US tensions
headlines.summary: Iran's World Cup squad arrived in Tijuana for the tournament as US-Iran tensions remain high. Several support staff were denied US visas while players received theirs.

perspectives.angle: Iran national soccer team arrives in Mexico for World Cup training
perspectives.summary: Iran squad lands in Mexico ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where they will prepare before group matches in the United States. The team faces US, England, and Ukraine in the group stage.

headlines.headline: Iran Suspends Flights at Tehran Airport Amid Escalation
headlines.summary: Iran civil aviation authority suspended all flights at Tehran International Airport until further notice, as regional tensions spike following Iran missile launches against Israel.

headlines.headline: China Expands Nuclear Arsenal to 620 Warheads: SIPRI
headlines.summary: Swedish think-tank SIPRI reports China has increased its nuclear warheads to 620 and may be increasing deployment as the New Start treaty expiry ends arms control efforts.

headlines.headline: Armenia Votes: Pashinyan Seeks Mandate for Westward Shift
headlines.summary: Armenians head to polls in parliamentary elections that could determine whether PM Pashinyan gets a mandate strong enough to reorient the country toward Europe amid tensions with Russia.

headlines.headline: UK Foreign Minister Calls for De-escalation Between Iran and Israel
headlines.summary: UK Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper urged both Iran and Israel to show restraint and de-escalate after Iran renewed missile attacks on Israel.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel, Violating Ceasefire Agreement
headlines.summary: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since the April ceasefire, marking a major escalation. Israel's military confirmed the attack, while Iran's state broadcaster acknowledged the strikes.

perspectives.angle: Iran fires missiles at Israel for first time since April ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs on Sunday, prompting Iran to launch missiles at Israeli territory. The attack marks a major escalation after weeks of fragile ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping Visits North Korea as China Seeks to Counter US Influence
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a state visit, declaring China's unwavering policy to upgrade ties with North Korea. The summit signals Beijing's strategic focus on regional security amid tensions with Washington.

headlines.headline: European Leaders Urge Urgent Action to Bolster Ukraine's Missile Defenses
headlines.summary: The leaders of the UK, France, Germany and Ukraine met to discuss ramping up weapons production to counter Russian ballistic missiles, as Zelensky warned Ukraine is close to developing its own ballistic missile capability.

perspectives.angle: European leaders gather in London to coordinate Ukraine support
perspectives.summary: British PM Keir Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron, and Merz for talks on continued backing for Ukraine as the war enters its fifth year with no end in sight.

headlines.headline: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Despite Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: OPEC+ agreed to increase oil production targets for the fourth consecutive month, even as the US-Iran conflict continues to block shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, affecting several member states' ability to pump more.

perspectives.angle: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
perspectives.summary: OPEC+ agreed to its fourth oil production increase in as many months despite the ongoing US-Iran conflict, approving a 188,000 barrel-per-day hike to compensate for Hormuz Strait disruptions.

headlines.headline: France Warns of New Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Within Days
headlines.summary: France's foreign minister announced that additional sanctions on Israeli settlers could be imposed in coming days, protesting the escalation of illegal settlements in the West Bank and rising settler violence against Palestinians.

perspectives.angle: France threatens more sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
perspectives.summary: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot warns that additional sanctions could be imposed on Israeli settlers in coming days in response to illegal settlement expansion and violence against Palestinians.

headlines.headline: Kosovo's Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority to End Crisis
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Albin Kurti's party won the most votes in Kosovo's third election in a year, but fell short of a majority amid a protracted political crisis.

perspectives.angle: Kosovo PM Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority
perspectives.summary: Albin Kurtis Vetevendosje party won nearly 44% of the vote in Kosovo parliamentary election, but fell short of a majority needed to end the ongoing political crisis.

headlines.headline: Armenia's Pro-Western Party Leads Election as Pashinyan Defies Russia
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract party is leading with 54% in Armenia's parliamentary election, a shift away from Russian influence toward the West.

perspectives.angle: Kosovo Election: Kurti Party Leads With 43.72%
perspectives.summary: Preliminary results show Prime Minister Albin Kurti Vetevendosje party leading Kosovo early parliamentary election, the third vote in 18 months amid political impasse.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects US Plan to Use Frozen Assets for Gulf Allies' Damages
headlines.summary: Tehran dismissed Washington's plan to use Iranian assets to compensate Gulf states for war damages, calling it unacceptable and warning regional governments.

perspectives.angle: US considers redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf allies for reconstruction
perspectives.summary: The Trump administration is exploring using frozen Iranian assets to fund rebuilding efforts in Gulf states affected by the conflict, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled over Tehran demand for access to $24 billion in oil revenue.

perspectives.angle: Iran Launches Ballistic Missiles Toward Gulf Allies as US Intercepts
perspectives.summary: Iran fired ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain, with US forces intercepting most of them. The attack marks another escalation in the ongoing US-Iran conflict despite ceasefire talks.

headlines.headline: Peru Heads to Runoff as Sánchez Faces Fujimori in Polarized Race
headlines.summary: Peruvians vote in a presidential runoff between Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori, with EU observers deployed as the country faces deep political divisions.

headlines.headline: Canada's Carney Heads to Ireland, France Before G7 Summit
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney will deepen ties with European partners before attending the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, with focus on reducing global tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran Launches Missile Barrage at Israel, First Since Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel in retaliation for Israeli strikes on Beirut, marking the first such attack since the Middle East ceasefire. Israel's air defenses are actively intercepting the incoming threats.

headlines.headline: Nepal FM Signals Diplomatic Approach to India Border Row
headlines.summary: Nepal Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal said no boundary is too complex if tackled with an open heart, signaling a diplomatic push to resolve territorial disputes with India and strengthen bilateral ties.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang for summit with Kim Jong Un
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea as Kim Jong Un projects confidence with firm Russian ties and nuclear arsenal, showing little appetite for engagement with Washington.

headlines.headline: US considers buying Chagos Islands from Mauritius
headlines.summary: The White House is weighing a plan to purchase the strategically important Chagos Islands from Mauritius, the Telegraph reports.

headlines.headline: Central African Republic agrees to accept third-country deportees from US
headlines.summary: The Trump administration strikes a deal with Central African Republic to accept migrants from other countries deported by the US, the latest in a series of opaque agreements with African states.

headlines.headline: The Atlantic: Ukraine Not Losing, Russia Not Winning as Momentum Shifts
headlines.summary: Gulf states and others now understand that the Russian narrative is wrong. Ukrainians continue to resist as the war enters a new phase with drone warfare reshaping the front lines.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Asserts Maritime Rights as Regional Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: Taiwan emphasized that its rights over territorial waters and exclusive economic zone must not be violated by any country, amid heightened regional tensions in the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: CBS News: Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violation as Stalemate Continues
headlines.summary: US military reports shooting down four Iranian drones headed toward Strait of Hormuz overnight. Peace talks remain deadlocked as both sides exchange accusations.

headlines.headline: Female Navy Officers Fear Career Cap After Hegseth Cuts Women From Promotions
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed all women from a Navy promotion list, raising concerns about gender discrimination in the military and impacting career trajectories for female officers.

headlines.headline: UK Military Tearing Its Hair Out as Labour Dithering Endangers National Security
headlines.summary: Critics condemn Labour government failure to strengthen British Armed Forces while war rages in Europe, with calls for urgent defence investment amid growing Russian threat.

headlines.headline: South Africa's Ramaphosa vows crackdown on xenophobic violence amid migration tensions
headlines.summary: President Ramaphosa announced tougher immigration enforcement and penalties for employers hiring undocumented workers, while distancing the government from vigilante immigration checks.

perspectives.angle: Malawi Begins Evacuation of Nationals From South Africa Amid Xenophobia Crisis
perspectives.summary: The Government of Malawi announced a voluntary repatriation exercise for its nationals displaced in South Africa, with the first 150 citizens returning home as xenophobic violence spreads.

headlines.headline: Armenians vote in pivotal election with Russia-West tensions in focus
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Pashinyan's pro-Western ruling party faces opposition candidates favoring closer ties with Russia, in a vote that could reshape the South Caucasus country's geopolitical alignment.

perspectives.angle: Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Testing Westward Shift Amid Russian Pressure
perspectives.summary: Armenia holds parliamentary elections seen as a test of PM Pashinyan's pivot away from Russia, with six pro-Russian opposition candidates arrested the day before the vote.

perspectives.angle: Armenia Holds Election That Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
perspectives.summary: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that could pivot the country away from Russia toward closer Western ties, with Prime Minister Pashinyan seeking a mandate for constitutional reforms.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV draws 1.2 million to Madrid Mass, challenges Europe on Christian roots
headlines.summary: Over a million people filled Madrid's streets for Pope Leo's outdoor Mass, where he urged Europe to acknowledge Christianity's contributions to its cultural identity and said God stands with the poor.

perspectives.angle: Over one million gather in Madrid for Pope Leo XIV Mass
perspectives.summary: More than a million people filled Madrid streets and Plaza de Cibeles for Pope Leo XIV's main open-air Mass during his first papal visit to Spain, in what officials called the largest event of his week-long trip.

headlines.headline: Iran's negotiator threatens US bases and Israeli assets over Lebanon escalation
headlines.summary: Iran's top negotiator declared US bases and Israeli assets in the Middle East "legitimate targets" following Israel's strikes on Beirut and the US naval blockade of Iran.

headlines.headline: US pushes IAEA resolution demanding Iran account for bombed nuclear sites
headlines.summary: The US is lobbying countries on the IAEA Board to back a resolution requiring Iran to disclose what happened to its bombed nuclear facilities and enriched uranium stocks.

perspectives.angle: US Demands Iran Disclose Nuclear Sites and Uranium Stocks at IAEA
perspectives.summary: A US draft resolution before the UN nuclear watchdog board calls on Iran to provide precise information on its bombed nuclear sites and enriched uranium stocks, escalating diplomatic pressure amid ongoing ceasefire tensions.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Coast Guard Expels Chinese Ships from Restricted Waters
headlines.summary: Taiwan's coast guard expelled four Chinese government vessels that entered restricted waters off the island's south, with both sides exchanging warnings in a rare escalation of tensions across the Taiwan Strait.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan-China Coast Guard Standoff Near Pratas Islands
perspectives.summary: Taiwanese and Chinese Coast Guards engaged in a tense standoff near the strategically located Pratas Islands in the South China Sea, the second such incident in two weeks amid escalating maritime tensions.

headlines.headline: Pakistan-Administered Kashmir Court Clears Path for Refugee Elections
headlines.summary: The top court in Pakistan-administered Kashmir upheld 12 legislative seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees, clearing the way for upcoming elections in the disputed territory.

perspectives.angle: Dozens arrested in Pakistan-administered Kashmir crackdown on banned group
perspectives.summary: Pakistani police arrested dozens of supporters of an outlawed group in Pakistan-administered Kashmir on Saturday, part of a broader regional security crackdown.

headlines.headline: IDF reveals major Hezbollah command tunnel network in southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli military published footage of a Hezbollah tunnel system near Beaufort Castle, showing sophisticated infrastructure built with Iranian support for coordinating attacks.

headlines.headline: IRGC-linked media says Iran will respond to Israeli strikes on Beirut
headlines.summary: A source close to Irans Revolutionary Guard told Saberin news outlet that Iran will respond to Israels violation of the ceasefire following airstrikes in southern Beirut.

headlines.headline: US sending third-country deportees to Central African Republic
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has arranged for the impoverished Central African Republic to accept deportees from third countries amid widespread protests against US immigration policies.

headlines.headline: China activates flood emergency response in six regions amid heavy rain forecast
headlines.summary: Chinese authorities warned of rising river levels, flash floods and mountain torrents as rainstorms are expected to continue through Tuesday across six provinces.

headlines.headline: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburbs Days After US-Brokered Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel carried out strikes on Beiruts southern suburbs without warning, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure just days after a US-supported ceasefire plan was announced. The strikes risk escalating tensions as Hezbollah had rejected the ceasefire terms.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup Team Lands in Mexico Amid US Visa Dispute
headlines.summary: Irans soccer team arrived in Tijuana, Mexico for the World Cup as Tehran accused Washington of obstructing team preparations. The diplomatic row highlights tensions between the two nations even as they negotiate an end to the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Beijing sends largest patrol ship east of Taiwan after Japan-Philippine boundary talks
headlines.summary: China deployed its largest patrol vessel near Taiwan following Japan-Philippine maritime boundary discussions. Communist Party mouthpiece warned the two countries are becoming a "source of trouble" and putting regional peace at risk.

headlines.headline: Finland's Stubb urges Europe to step up and lead peace talks with Russia
headlines.summary: Finnish President Alexander Stubb called for Europe to take a more active role in leading peace negotiations with Russia, as the continent grapples with how to resolve the ongoing conflict in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: UK Royal Navy's entire submarine fleet out of service for maintenance
headlines.summary: Britain's entire fleet of Astute-class submarines are currently out of service waiting for maintenance, raising concerns about the UK's ability to deter Russian stealth submarines and protect its Vanguard fleet.

headlines.headline: Cabinet bodyguards working without security clearance
headlines.summary: Bodyguards protecting senior UK Cabinet ministers have been operating without proper security clearance, according to a leaked letter raising serious concerns about ministerial safety.

headlines.headline: Global airlines slash 2026 profit forecast on fuel shock from Iran war
headlines.summary: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) downgraded its 2026 profit outlook for the global airline industry, citing fuel price volatility and geopolitical disruption from the Iran conflict. Profits are expected to halve despite resilient passenger demand.

perspectives.angle: Global Airlines Slash 2026 Profit Forecast on Iran War Fuel Shock
perspectives.summary: The global airline industry nearly halved its 2026 profit forecast to $23 billion, citing fuel price volatility from the Middle East conflict. Middle East carriers are expected to post a $4.3 billion loss as passenger demand remains resilient.

headlines.headline: Trump rules out unfreezing Iranian assets before peace deal
headlines.summary: In an NBC News interview, US President Donald Trump stated he would not unfreeze Iranian assets or lift sanctions until a comprehensive peace agreement is reached. The stance could complicate ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Iran demands $24 billion in frozen assets as condition for US peace deal progress
perspectives.summary: Iranian military adviser Mohsen Rezaei said negotiations with the US have reached a deadlock, tying progress to the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets. Tensions remain high following US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

headlines.headline: One killed, five wounded in central Israel terror shooting spree
headlines.summary: A terrorist attack in communities bordering the West Bank north of Tel Aviv left one person dead and five wounded, with two in serious condition. One perpetrator was shot during the rampage.

perspectives.angle: One Dead, Four Wounded in Suspected Terror Shooting in Central Israel
perspectives.summary: A shooting attack in Kochav Ya'ir area left one person dead and at least four wounded; Israeli security forces captured one suspect and are searching for another.

headlines.headline: Bridge linking Crimea to southern Ukraine damaged in drone strike
headlines.summary: A drone strike damaged a bridge near the village of Chonhar connecting occupied Crimea with Russian-controlled parts of southern Ukraine, impacting a key logistics route for Russian forces.

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi summit on Taiwan offered more than optics
headlines.summary: The meeting between Chinese and US presidents laid the groundwork for more predictable China-US relations, including on the Taiwan issue, beyond just symbolic photo opportunities.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Strike Hits Nuclear Fuel Storage Near Chornobyl
headlines.summary: A Russian drone damaged a storage facility for spent nuclear fuel near the Chornobyl plant, Zelensky condemned as "extremely vile." The IAEA confirmed the strike hit near where nuclear material is stored, with no radiation spike detected.

perspectives.angle: Russian Strike Hits Bus Stop in Zaporizhzhia, Three Civilians Killed
perspectives.summary: A Russian strike on the settlement of Balabyne in Zaporizhzhia Oblast killed three people and injured three others. The attack targeted civilian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Pakistan's Interior Minister in Tehran to Mediate Iran-US Talks
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran carrying a special letter as regional powers push to revive Iran-US negotiations, while US forces downed additional Iranian drones over the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: Pakistani Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Iran-US Mediation Efforts
perspectives.summary: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to mediate between Iran and the United States as tensions continue to escalate in the Gulf region.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping makes rare state visit to North Korea for first time in nearly seven years
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping will visit Pyongyang for talks with Kim Jong Un, projecting confidence and defiance as North Korea maintains its nuclear arsenal and alliance with Russia while showing little interest in engaging Washington.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Declares Nuclear Program Irreversible Ahead of Xi Jinping Visit
perspectives.summary: Kim Yo-jong, sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, rejected US denuclearization efforts as 'anachronistic' just one day before Chinese President Xi Jinping's arrival in Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Russia downs 339 Ukrainian drones in massive 13-hour interception barrage
headlines.summary: Russian air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones over a 13-hour period across multiple regions including Moscow, in one of the largest drone attacks of the war.

perspectives.angle: Russia says it intercepted 339 Ukrainian drones over 13 hours
perspectives.summary: Russia's Defence Ministry reported intercepting and destroying 339 Ukrainian drones over a 13-hour period across multiple Russian regions, including Moscow, in one of the largest drone barrages of the war.

headlines.headline: Russia signals openness to new US relationship as Ukraine war stalls
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has expressed openness to a new relationship with the United States, even as peace talks show no progress in ending the Ukraine conflict. The overture comes amid continued battlefield deadlock.

perspectives.angle: Russia signals openness to new relationship with US amid Ukraine deadlock
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has sought to project a bullish view of Russia's position even as talks appear no closer to ending the war. The overture comes as Ukraine continues drone strikes deep into Russian territory.

headlines.headline: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power supply after outage
headlines.summary: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its power supply Saturday after an outage, according to plant management. The facility has faced repeated power disruptions during the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power supply after emergency
perspectives.summary: The Russian-installed management of Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant said it restored the Ferrosplavnaya-1 power line that supplies electricity to the facility, amid ongoing nuclear safety concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigerian military rescues 360 hostages from Borno mountain hideout
headlines.summary: Security forces freed 360 men, women, and children held by kidnappers in a mountain enclave in Borno state, in a major operation against militant groups.

perspectives.angle: Nigerian Army Rescues 360 Hostages from Boko Haram Stronghold
perspectives.summary: Nigerian forces freed 360 kidnapped victims from a Boko Haram enclave in the Mandara Mountains after weeks of intelligence-gathering and surveillance operations.

headlines.headline: Greek police arrest Palestinian man on Crete over Hamas-linked terror plot
headlines.summary: A 37-year-old Palestinian was detained on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and receiving training abroad, linked to four similar arrests in Cyprus.

perspectives.angle: Arrested Palestinian in Crete Confesses Hamas Membership, Training in Indonesia
perspectives.summary: Greek counter-terrorism officers obtained a full confession from the 37-year-old Palestinian arrested on Crete, who admitted to Hamas membership and training in Indonesia.

headlines.headline: Armenia arrests six opposition candidates day before election
headlines.summary: Armenian authorities detained six candidates from a pro-Russian opposition party on Saturday, one day before parliamentary elections seen as a test of the government's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

headlines.headline: Pakistan delivers special letter to Iran's supreme leader
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi traveled to Tehran with messages from the prime minister and army chief as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the US-Iran conflict and prevent further escalation in the Gulf.

headlines.headline: Greek police arrest Palestinian man suspected of Hamas attack plot
headlines.summary: Police on the Greek island of Crete arrested a 37-year-old Palestinian man on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and belonging to the militant group Hamas, authorities said.

headlines.headline: European Central Bank Begins Interest Rate Hikes
headlines.summary: The ECB starts raising interest rates, marking a significant shift in European monetary policy as the continent grapples with economic pressures from ongoing geopolitical tensions.

headlines.headline: Singapore Blocks Anti-Indian Content, Traces Origin to China
headlines.summary: Singapore authorities invoke the Online Criminal Harms Act to restrict access to posts targeting the Indian community, saying the content likely originated from China-based sources.

headlines.headline: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance in Congress
headlines.summary: Republican lawmakers are increasingly breaking with Trump on key policy issues including the Iran conflict as midterm election pressures mount, potentially threatening his agenda.

perspectives.angle: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance as Midterm Pressures Mount
perspectives.summary: Republican lawmakers are showing increased willingness to break with Trump on key initiatives as midterm election pressures intensify, with both parties skeptical of claims of an actual revolt.

headlines.headline: UN: Hormuz Closure Affects 1 Billion Lives
headlines.summary: The UN Conference on Trade and Development reports that 65 of 75 least developed countries depend on imported oil, with the Hormuz disruption threatening nearly one billion people with rising energy costs and difficult trade-offs.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Plans ChatGPT Superapp Overhaul Ahead of Potential IPO
headlines.summary: OpenAI is planning its biggest ChatGPT overhaul yet, aiming to turn it into a "superapp" with coding tools and AI agents to boost revenue ahead of a potential stock market listing, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.

headlines.headline: Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Under Russian Pressure
headlines.summary: Armenians are voting in parliamentary elections as the government seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and increase cooperation with the West, facing Russian pressure aimed at preventing a drift away from its sphere of influence.

headlines.headline: Airbus Closing In on Widebody Order From Scandinavian Airlines
headlines.summary: Airbus is closing in on a widebody order from SAS, including A330neo and A350 aircraft, with 15 to 20 planes under consideration, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday.

headlines.headline: Boeing Can Support China for 200-Jet Order, Executive Says
headlines.summary: Boeing can provide aftermarket parts support to China for a 200-plane order announced following Trump's visit to Beijing earlier this year, a top services executive said on Saturday.

headlines.headline: High Fuel Costs Could Trigger Airline Failures and Consolidation
headlines.summary: Budget carriers have been among the hardest hit by elevated jet fuel prices linked to the Iran war, and the industry chief warned of potential failures and consolidation ahead.

headlines.headline: ECB Set to Raise Interest Rates for First Time Since 2022
headlines.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde is expected to announce a 0.25% rate increase to 2.25% on Thursday, driven by inflation from the Iran conflict. The move will impact 110,000 Irish tracker mortgage holders and raise borrowing costs across the eurozone.

headlines.headline: India Raises LPG Prices Amid Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Domestic cooking gas prices increased by Rs 29 per cylinder in Delhi, bringing the cost to Rs 942. This marks the second hike since West Asia tensions disrupted global energy supplies, with state oil companies still reporting losses of Rs 703 per cylinder sold.

headlines.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza kills seven Palestinians including two women
headlines.summary: An Israeli strike killed at least seven Palestinians in Gaza on Saturday as mediators restarted talks in Cairo with Hamas over safeguarding a strained ceasefire agreement.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Strike Kills Three Lebanese Soldiers Including Two Officers
perspectives.summary: An Israeli strike on a military vehicle in southern Lebanon killed two Lebanese army officers and one soldier, with the Israeli military saying it is investigating the incident amid ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Erdogan shifts balance of power as Putin becomes increasingly dependent on Turkey
headlines.summary: The Ukraine war has shifted the dynamic between Putin and Erdogan. Isolated by the West, Putin increasingly relies on the Turkish president who was once the junior partner.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike railway infrastructure in occupied Luhansk and Donetsk
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces conducted drone strikes on energy and transport infrastructure in Russian-occupied Luhansk and Donetsk oblasts overnight, targeting logistical networks supporting Russian military operations.

headlines.headline: Rosneft chief says US energy companies benefit from Hormuz closure
headlines.summary: Rosneft CEO Igor Sechin said US energy companies are the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure, but warned continued tensions would undermine long-term oil demand.

perspectives.angle: Rosneft Chief: US Energy Companies Benefit Most From Hormuz Closure
perspectives.summary: Russian oil giant Rosneft's CEO Igor Sechin said US energy companies were the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure but warned continued tensions would undermine long-term oil demand.

headlines.headline: South Korean protesters demand election re-run after ballot shortage
headlines.summary: Protesters rallied for a second day outside a ballot-counting site in South Korea, demanding a re-run of local elections after ballot shortages disrupted voting earlier in the week.

perspectives.angle: South Korean Protesters Demand Election Re-Run After Ballot Shortage
perspectives.summary: Protesters in South Korea rallied for a second day outside a ballot-counting site, demanding a re-run of local elections held earlier this week after reports of ballot shortages disrupted voting.

headlines.headline: Hormuz crisis exposes global trade vulnerability
headlines.summary: Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz demonstrated how a single chokepoint can be weaponized to disrupt global energy supplies and trade flows.

headlines.headline: Taiwan reports first coordinated Chinese coast guard and survey ship operation near South China Sea islands
headlines.summary: Taiwan accused China of carrying out its first coordinated coast guard and survey ship operation near strategically located islands in the South China Sea, calling it a provocation.

headlines.headline: Armenia arrests six pro-Russian opposition candidates day before election
headlines.summary: Armenian authorities arrested six candidates from a pro-Russian opposition party on Saturday, one day before parliamentary elections that could reshape the country's ties with Moscow and the West.

headlines.headline: Missing American student found dead near Kyoto, Japan
headlines.summary: An American student who disappeared while on a family vacation in Japan was found dead outside Kyoto, his mother confirmed.

perspectives.angle: Missing US Student Found Dead in Japan
perspectives.summary: The body of James Higginbotham, 20, was discovered in a mountainous area near Kyoto after he disappeared while on a family trip to Japan. Local authorities are investigating the circumstances of his death.

headlines.headline: Israel struck 150 Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon in 48 hours
headlines.summary: Israeli military reports intensive strikes on Hezbollah positions as cross-border fighting intensifies.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon Opens Second International Airport Amid Israel-Hezbollah War
perspectives.summary: Lebanon inaugurated Qlayaat airport near the Syrian border as a second international hub, converting it from military airbase to passenger use as the country seeks to shift traffic from Beirut amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia condemns Israeli aggression against Lebanon
headlines.summary: Riyadh issued a strong condemnation of Israeli military operations in Lebanon amid escalating regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia Condemns Israeli Aggression Against Lebanon
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia issued a strong condemnation of what it called Israeli aggression against Lebanon, adding regional pressure amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.

headlines.headline: Lebanon army chief visits Pakistan as US-Iran mediator
headlines.summary: Lebanon military chief traveled to Pakistan for talks as Islamabad mediates between Washington and Tehran in the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Airbus Nearing Widebody Order From Scandinavian Airlines
headlines.summary: Airbus is close to a widebody order from SAS AB including A330neo and A350 aircraft, with 15-20 planes under consideration, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday.

headlines.headline: Musk SpaceX IPO Draws European Retail Investor Interest
headlines.summary: European retail investors are among those jockeying for a piece of the anticipated SpaceX initial public offering, though observers warn the deal could prove bumpy for those without institutional resources.

perspectives.angle: SpaceX IPO draws $150 billion in demand, twice the offering size
perspectives.summary: Investor demand for SpaceX's $75 billion IPO has reached approximately $150 billion, making it roughly two times oversubscribed ahead of the highly anticipated market debut.

headlines.headline: Five Killed in Russian Attacks on Southern Kherson Region
headlines.summary: Russian forces killed five civilians in three separate attacks on Ukraine southern Kherson region on Friday, as fighting continues despite ongoing ceasefire discussions.

perspectives.angle: Five Civilians Killed in Russian Attacks on Kherson Region
perspectives.summary: Russian forces killed five people in three separate attacks on Ukraine's southern Kherson region on Friday, according to the regional governor, as fighting continues in the occupied territories.

headlines.headline: Maine Senate Candidate Platner Faces Intimidation Allegations
headlines.summary: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies reports of physical intimidation toward women as scandals shadow the Maine Democratic primary race.

headlines.headline: US Envoy: Taiwan Must Spend Smarter on Defense, Learn from Ukraine and Middle East
headlines.summary: A senior US diplomat urged Taiwan to optimize defense spending and adopt drone warfare lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts to maintain the military balance with China.

headlines.headline: French Telecom Consortium Signs $23.4 Billion Deal to Buy SFR from Altice
headlines.summary: Bouygues Telecom, Orange, and Free-iliad signed an agreement to acquire telecom operator SFR from Altice France in one of Europe largest telecom deals.

headlines.headline: Secret Service-Level Security as Trump to Attend NBA Finals Game 3
headlines.summary: Road closures, bag bans and extensive security measures will greet fans at Madison Square Garden as President Trump attends Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs.

headlines.headline: US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down four Iranian attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz, marking another escalation in the fragile ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

perspectives.angle: US shoots down Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz as conflict enters 100th day
perspectives.summary: American forces intercepted Iranian drones threatening international shipping in the strategic waterway, while Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed three high-ranking Lebanese soldiers.

headlines.headline: Former French First Lady Bernadette Chirac Dies at 93
headlines.summary: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace beside President Jacques Chirac, has died. She was known for weathering political challenges and her husband's infidelities with dignity.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes St Petersburg After Putin Rejects Zelensky Meeting
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted St Petersburg after Putin rejected a proposed meeting with Zelensky. The attacks came on the final day of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, with one person killed and an oil depot set ablaze.

headlines.headline: India's Viral "Cockroach" Youth Movement Holds First Major Protest
headlines.summary: Hundreds joined a protest led by India's mock "Cockroach Janta Party," a youth movement with roughly 22 million Instagram followers that has become the largest online expression of dissent against the government's policies.

perspectives.angle: India Youth March Against Exam Leaks in Delhi Cockroaches Protest
perspectives.summary: Thousands of young Indians protested in New Delhi under the angry cockroaches movement, demanding accountability for exam paper leaks that have undermined educational opportunities and government job prospects.

headlines.headline: US Approves .5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
headlines.summary: The United States approved the sale of five maritime helicopters to New Zealand worth .5 billion, as Wellington moves to nearly double its military spending amid growing regional security concerns.

perspectives.angle: US Approves $1.98 Billion Counter-Drone Defense Sale to Kuwait
perspectives.summary: The State Department approved a potential $1.98 billion arms sale to Kuwait for counter-UAV systems including Roadrunner-Munition and Anvil-Kinetic interceptors, strengthening Gulf defenses against drone threats.

headlines.headline: US Energy Secretary: Lower Gas Prices Require Resolution With Iran
headlines.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that lowering pump prices will ultimately require a resolution with Iran to restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, linking domestic energy costs to the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Cuba's Raul Castro Makes First Public Appearance Since US Indictment
headlines.summary: Former Cuban leader Raul Castro appeared at an Interior Ministry event in Havana, his first public appearance since being indicted on murder charges in the United States last month.

headlines.headline: Seven-month-old Palestinian baby buried after Israeli forces shooting
headlines.summary: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old Palestinian baby boy who was killed when Israeli troops fired at the vehicle he was in during a military operation in the occupied West Bank, according to medical officials.

perspectives.angle: Israeli forces kill Palestinian infant in West Bank shooting
perspectives.summary: A seven-month-old Palestinian baby was killed and his parents wounded when Israeli soldiers opened fire on their vehicle in Hebron. The Israeli military said the incident is under review and those injured were uninvolved civilians.

headlines.headline: Iranian students protest nationwide over exam changes, arrests reported
headlines.summary: Students across Iran demonstrate against changes to university entrance exams, chanting slogans demanding justice and education rights. First significant domestic protests since the US-Iran conflict began.

headlines.headline: WHO warns of nearly 500 Ebola cases in Central Africa outbreak
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization reports 100 new cases and 20 additional deaths in the Central Africa Ebola outbreak, raising alarm about the epidemic scale. Cases rose from 400 to nearly 500 in one week.

headlines.headline: Japan to designate Philippines as top priority for oil reserve support
headlines.summary: Tokyo plans to work with JICA starting this summer to provide oil reserve support to the Philippines, deepening energy cooperation amid regional tensions with China in the South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Nancy Mace breaks with Trump over Epstein vote in heated primary battle
headlines.summary: Congresswoman Nancy Mace faces consequences from Trump for her vote on Jeffrey Epstein-related legislation as she navigates a contentious primary challenge. The vote has become a flashpoint in her re-election campaign.

headlines.headline: Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schroeder
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder in a one-on-one meeting described as "good and friendly" by the Kremlin, amid ongoing tensions over the war in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schröder
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met one-on-one with Gerhard Schröder in what the Kremlin described as a good and friendly encounter, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

headlines.headline: Senior US Diplomat Urges Taiwan to Spend Smarter on Defense
headlines.summary: Taiwan needs to "spend smarter" on defense and learn from the Ukraine and Middle East wars on using drones to counter China, the de facto U.S. ambassador to Taipei said.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Needs to Spend Smarter on Defense, US Diplomat Says
perspectives.summary: Taiwan must "spend smarter" on defense and learn lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts about drone warfare to maintain the military balance with China, the de facto US ambassador to Taipei said.

headlines.headline: White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
headlines.summary: White House AI policy adviser Krishnan is leaving his position, according to information reports, at a time of increasing focus on artificial intelligence policy.

headlines.headline: Defense Secretary Hegseth Links Immigration to Invasion in D-Day Speech
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech in France to draw parallels between WWII and current immigration challenges, calling immigration by sea an invasion that threatens nations.

headlines.headline: US Stocks Tumble After Strong Jobs Data Signals Higher Rates
headlines.summary: The S&P 500 fell more than 2.6% on Friday, its worst one-day drop of the year. An unexpectedly strong May jobs report bolstered bets that US interest rates are poised to rise, knocking tech stocks lower.

headlines.headline: 100 Days Into Iran War, Trump Struggles to Rally Domestic Support
headlines.summary: As the US-Iran conflict reaches the 100-day mark, analysts note growing war weariness among Americans. The unpopularity of the conflict may impact Republican electoral prospects and US policy decisions.

perspectives.angle: 100 Days of Iran War: More Ceasefire Days Than Combat
perspectives.summary: Newsweek analyzes the first 100 days of the Iran conflict by numbers, showing more days of ceasefire than active fighting even as tensions persist.

headlines.headline: Albanians Protest Jared Kushner-Backed Luxury Resort on Nature Reserve
headlines.summary: Thousands of Albanians protested plans for a luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner on the country's coast, raising concerns about environmental impact on protected areas.

headlines.headline: Singapore Orders Social Media Platforms to Block Foreign Posts Targeting Indian Community
headlines.summary: Singapore's government directed social media platforms to block foreign-originated content targeting the Indian community, invoking new online harm prevention laws.

perspectives.angle: Singapore orders social media platforms to block posts targeting Indian community
perspectives.summary: Authorities directed YouTube, Facebook, and X to block 14 foreign posts that targeted the Indian community. Officials said the content undermined social cohesion.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Accuses China of Coordinated Operation Near Strategic South China Sea Islands
headlines.summary: A Chinese coast guard ship and survey vessel carried out what Taiwan called the first coordinated provocation near strategically located islands in the South China Sea.

perspectives.angle: China Deploys New Missiles Matching Patriot Capability Near Taiwan
perspectives.summary: State broadcaster CCTV showed troops from the Fujian-based 73rd Army Group testing new missiles in the Gobi Desert. The deployment represents a significant enhancement of Beijing's missile forces opposite Taiwan.

headlines.headline: White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
headlines.summary: White House AI policy adviser Sriram Krishnan plans to leave his position at the end of June, the Information reported on Saturday.

headlines.headline: Jamaica Experiences Island-Wide Power Blackout
headlines.summary: Jamaica is experiencing a nationwide power outage, with the energy minister confirming the blackout on social media. No cause has been given yet.

headlines.headline: Trump Urges Firing of Intelligence Community Employees
headlines.summary: President Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants the acting director of national intelligence to begin firing large numbers of employees as part of a shake-up of the US intelligence community.

perspectives.angle: Trump Urges Mass Firings at US Intelligence Agencies
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants acting DNI Bill Pulte to begin firing large numbers of intelligence community employees as part of a major shake-up of US spy agencies.

headlines.headline: Meta Considers Major Stock Offering for AI Infrastructure
headlines.summary: Meta is weighing raising tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to fund AI infrastructure ambitions, according to Financial Times. The move comes as tech giants race to expand AI capabilities.

headlines.headline: Hamas Says Cairo Talks Begin on Gaza Ceasefire Implementation
headlines.summary: Hamas announced that talks in Cairo are focusing on ending Israeli violations, opening crossings, delivering aid, and completing the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Explosion at Myanmar building storing mining explosives kills over 45
headlines.summary: A blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar near the Chinese border killed more than 45 people and injured 70 others. The Taang National Liberation Army said gelignite stored for mining operations detonated.

headlines.headline: At least 49 migrants die of thirst in Niger desert after truck breakdown
headlines.summary: Forty-nine people died of thirst in the Sahara desert after their truck broke down while traveling from Mali to Niger for Eid al-Adha celebrations. Two survivors walked more than 50 km to alert authorities.

perspectives.angle: 49 Migrants Die of Thirst in Niger After Truck Breaks Down in Sahara Desert
perspectives.summary: Forty-nine people died of thirst when a truck broke down in a remote part of Niger's Sahara desert, highlighting the deadly risks faced by migrants crossing the region.

headlines.headline: Major fire breaks out at one of Russias biggest oil refineries
headlines.summary: A large blaze broke out at the Antipinsky Oil Refinery in Tyumen, one of Russias biggest private refineries, according to local reports.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip in US Negotiations
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun made his toughest criticism yet of Tehran, accusing Iran of leveraging Lebanon in its talks with Washington while Hezbollah wages war with Israel. The statement highlights growing Arab frustration with Iranian interference.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon President Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip
perspectives.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accused Iran of using Lebanon as a bargaining chip in its negotiations with the United States, in some of his toughest criticism yet of Tehran and its Lebanese ally Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Macron, Starmer, Merz to Meet Zelenskiy in London for War Talks
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, June 7, as European leaders seek to support Ukraine amid stalled peace efforts.

perspectives.angle: Macron, Starmer and Merz to meet Zelenskiy in London for Ukraine talks
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, according to the French presidency.

perspectives.angle: Macron, Starmer, Merz to Meet Zelensky in London on June 7
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in London on Sunday. The meeting signals continued European coordination on Ukraine support.

headlines.headline: NASA Orders ISS Astronauts to Shelter During Russian Module Air Leak Repair
headlines.summary: Five astronauts aboard the International Space Station took shelter and prepared for evacuation as Russian cosmonauts attempted to fix a worsening crack in the Zvezda service module. NASA later reversed the evacuation order after repairs.

headlines.headline: Taipei Zoo Welcomes First Red Pandas From China in Over a Decade
headlines.summary: Taiwan received red pandas from a zoo in Fujian province, marking a rare instance of animal diplomacy amid cross-strait tensions and the first such exchange since 2014.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires Missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait, Escalating Regional Conflict
headlines.summary: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait on Saturday, with Bahrain intercepting the attacks. Kuwait condemned the strikes as a dangerous escalation threatening Gulf stability.

perspectives.angle: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel After Beirut Strike
perspectives.summary: Israel reported Iran launched missiles toward Israeli territory after Israel struck Beirut, marking a dangerous escalation following the fragile ceasefire. The attack raises fears of wider regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest POW Swap
headlines.summary: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel on Friday in the latest prisoner of war swap, continuing a pattern of exchanges amid the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine and Russia exchange 185 prisoners each in latest swap
perspectives.summary: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel in the latest prisoner of war swap, officials in both countries said, continuing regular exchanges despite ongoing hostilities.

headlines.headline: US companies to add 40 million barrels to Strategic Petroleum Reserve after Iran war
headlines.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced that companies borrowing oil from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve will add back an extra 40 million barrels as premiums once the Iran conflict ends, addressing concerns about depleted domestic reserves.

headlines.headline: US oil exports surge, draining Cushing inventories toward historic lows
headlines.summary: US oil exports have reached record levels as refiners worldwide scramble to fill the supply gap from Middle East war disruptions, leaving storage tanks at Cushing, Oklahoma nearly empty and raising concerns about domestic supply security.

headlines.headline: US sanctions network smuggling Iranian LPG to Asia disguised as Omani product
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a network of individuals, entities, and tankers smuggling Iranian-origin liquid petroleum gas to South and East Asia while falsely labeling it as Omani LPG.

headlines.headline: Pentagon flags Israel as highest counterintelligence risk amid spying concerns
headlines.summary: The US Defense Intelligence Agency raised Israel's threat rating to critical, citing surveillance concerns. Officials report using burner phones and taking extra precautions during visits.

headlines.headline: Iran seizes US-linked vessel in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's Fars News Agency reported the seizure of a vessel belonging to a US-based shipping company in the strategic waterway, escalating maritime tensions.

headlines.headline: Ireland scrutinizes Russian-linked alumina plant as sanctions pressure mounts
headlines.summary: The Aughinish Alumina facility in Limerick faces renewed attention over its connections to Russia's military supply chain, as Europe debates sanctions enforcement.

headlines.headline: US lawmakers raise concerns over civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
headlines.summary: Congress members expressed worries about uranium enrichment provisions in the proposed agreement, with experts noting it may not require adherence to nonproliferation standards.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup Players Granted US Visas, Some Staff Still Waiting
headlines.summary: Irans World Cup soccer players received visas to enter the United States days before their first match, though some administrative staff remain without visas.

perspectives.angle: Iran Condemns US Over World Cup Visa Denials for Delegation Staff
perspectives.summary: Iran accused the US of discriminatory treatment after World Cup host nation refused visas for some delegation support staff, while players received their visas just 10 days before their opening match.

headlines.headline: Iran Reaffirms Support for Hezbollah as Broader Peace Deal Falters
headlines.summary: Iran declared continued backing for Hezbollah and demanded Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, complicating interim negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

perspectives.angle: Iran reaffirms support for Hezbollah as broader peace deal faces complications
perspectives.summary: Iran has reaffirmed support for its Lebanese ally Hezbollah and demanded Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon, underscoring the complications facing an interim deal to end the broader conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Open Letter to Putin Aimed at Wider Audience Beyond Moscow
headlines.summary: Ukraines president offered face-to-face peace talks in an open letter that analysts say was designed to resonate at the St. Petersburg investment forum and beyond.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Appeals Directly to Putin in Open Letter Calling for Face-to-Face Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky published an unprecedented open letter to Vladimir Putin, proposing direct negotiations in a neutral country to end the war. The letter comes as Ukraine reports gaining more territory than it lost for the third consecutive month.

headlines.headline: France to Test AI-Powered Battlefield Command in NATO Exercise
headlines.summary: The French Army has developed its own large-language model called Berthier for staff officers, named after Napoleon's chief of staff. The system will be tested in June NATO exercises as militaries increasingly adopt AI for command decisions.

headlines.headline: UAE Markets Rise Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Dubai's stock market outperformed regional peers even as hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict faded, with investors appearing to price in limited escalation risks.

headlines.headline: Trump's 'Crazy' Rebuke Undercuts Netanyahu at Critical Moment
headlines.summary: President Trump's public criticism of Prime Minister Netanyahu over Gaza policy has exposed a rift at a crucial time, complicating Israel's position as it navigates ceasefires with both Iran and Hezbollah.

perspectives.angle: Trump's 'crazy' rebuke undercuts Netanyahu at critical moment
perspectives.summary: Trump's public criticism of Netanyahu as 'crazy' has undercut the Israeli leader at a delicate moment, highlighting tensions despite Netanyahu's portrayal of being uniquely adept at dealing with the US president.

headlines.headline: European Shares Slip as Tech Rally Stalls, Middle East Weighs
headlines.summary: European stocks ended the week lower as technology stocks paused their two-month rally and Middle East uncertainty kept investors cautious, with major indices posting weekly losses.

headlines.headline: Sea drone self-detonates near Romanian Black Sea port amid Russian jamming
headlines.summary: A sea drone self-destructed near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties, as Ukraine said Russian jamming caused the vessel to drift off course.

headlines.headline: Russian drone attack kills four at dairy factory in Kyiv region
headlines.summary: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in the Ukrainian capital region killed four people and injured seven more, Ukrainian officials said.

perspectives.angle: Russian drone strikes kill four at Kyiv dairy factory
perspectives.summary: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in the Kyiv region killed four people and injured seven others, Ukrainian officials reported.

headlines.headline: Bank of Canada expected to hold rates through 2026 despite inflation risks
headlines.summary: The Bank of Canada will hold its key overnight rate at 2.25% on June 10 and for the rest of the year, according to a Reuters poll, despite rising inflation risks from conflict-driven energy prices.

perspectives.angle: ECB Expected to Hike Rates Next Week as Iran War Fuels Inflation Pressures
perspectives.summary: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates, becoming the first major central bank to do so since the Iran conflict unleashed an energy crisis stoking inflationary pressures in the euro zone.

headlines.headline: UN Security Council to convene emergency meeting on Russian missile terror
headlines.summary: Ukraine requested an emergency UN Security Council meeting for June 8 in response to Russia recent large-scale ballistic missile attacks on Ukrainian cities. Foreign Minister Sybiha said Moscow continues choosing escalation over peace.

headlines.headline: Iran FM rejects Lebanese interference accusations, says focus on real foe
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi dismissed Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accusation of interference, telling him to focus on the real foe as Lebanon leaders call for Iran to stop interfering in their affairs.

headlines.headline: China mobilizes SOEs and tech firms to hire record 12.7 million graduates
headlines.summary: Eight government departments launched a nationwide campaign requiring state-owned enterprises and major private tech firms to expand hiring for the class of 2026, marking one of China broadest labor market interventions in recent years.

headlines.headline: KMT leader says Taiwan can preserve peace by avoiding de jure independence
headlines.summary: Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun told a Harvard seminar that cross-strait peace can be maintained as long as Taipei does not cross the red line of de jure independence, advocating for both deterrence and dialogue frameworks.

headlines.headline: Putin says he sees no reason to meet Zelenskiy after Ukrainian peace overture
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he currently saw no reason to meet Volodymyr Zelenskiy after the Ukrainian president published an open letter proposing face-to-face talks to end the war now in its fifth year.

headlines.headline: Turkish fishing boat attacked in Black Sea, one sailor killed
headlines.summary: A Turkish-flagged fishing boat was attacked and sank off the northern Black Sea coast, leaving one sailor dead and four wounded. The Turkish Coast Guard is investigating the incident.

perspectives.angle: Turkish trawler sinks in Black Sea after apparent attack
perspectives.summary: A Turkish-flagged fishing vessel sank in the Black Sea following an apparent attack, leaving one crew member dead amid rising regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Putin holds friendly meeting with former German Chancellor Schroeder
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a one-on-one meeting with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, described as "good and friendly" by Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

headlines.headline: Canada reports strong job growth with unemployment rate falling to 6.6%
headlines.summary: Canada's economy added 87,800 jobs in May with unemployment falling to 6.6%, defying expectations and showing economic resilience despite signs of slower growth.

headlines.headline: Iran Dodges US Blockade Through Iraq as New Smuggling Route Emerges
headlines.summary: More than 50 days after the US-led maritime blockade, Iraq's Umm Qasr port has become a key smuggling route for Iran to circumvent sanctions.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Exchange Strikes in Gulf as Ceasefire Efforts Stall
headlines.summary: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down drones near the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain in a new escalation complicating peace efforts.

perspectives.angle: Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violations as Both Sides Exchange Strikes
perspectives.summary: Iran accused the US of violating ceasefire terms after American forces shot down Iranian drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz and intercepted ballistic missiles targeting Kuwait and Bahrain.

headlines.headline: UAE Stock Markets Rally Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Dubai markets outperformed regional peers on Friday, with investors appearing to brush aside fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China Invests $162 Million in AI to Promote Xi Jinping Ideology
headlines.summary: Xinhuanet plans to invest over 1.1 billion yuan on an authoritative AI agent to help promote President Xi Jinping political thinking, according to Shanghai Stock Exchange filings.

perspectives.angle: China Plans $162 Million AI System to Promote Xi Jinping's Political Thinking
perspectives.summary: State-owned Xinhuanet plans to invest over 1.1 billion yuan in an "authoritative" AI agent to promote President Xi Jinping's ideology, marking a new frontier in the Communist Party's use of technology for political messaging.

headlines.headline: China's Xi Jinping to Visit North Korea in First Trip in Nearly 7 Years
headlines.summary: China announced President Xi Jinping will visit North Korea on a two-day trip from June 8, his first in nearly seven years, as Beijing seeks to reassert ties with Pyongyang, its only formal treaty ally.

perspectives.angle: Xi Jinping to visit North Korea next week in first trip since 2019
perspectives.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries announced Friday, in what will be his first visit since 2019. The meeting comes amid shifting regional dynamics and US-China tensions.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President Aoun Thanks Saudi Crown Prince for Support
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun spoke by phone with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, thanking him for Saudi support to Beirut amid ongoing regional tensions and humanitarian crisis.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Crown Prince condemns Iranian attacks on Bahrain in call with King Hamad
perspectives.summary: Mohammed bin Salman expressed solidarity with Bahrain during a phone call with King Hamad, condemning Iranian attacks and underscoring regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UNESCO Body Recommends Japan Ancient Sites for Heritage List
headlines.summary: A UNESCO advisory body has recommended adding the Asuka-Fujiwara ancient sites in Nara Prefecture to the World Heritage list, highlighting Japan historical heritage recognition.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone damages Romanian Black Sea port terminal
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian maritime drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Constanta port after Russia jammed the vessel, causing it to drift off course. No casualties reported.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Maritime Drone Explodes at Romanian Black Sea Port
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian maritime drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties. Ukraine stated Russia jammed the vessel, causing it to drift off course.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall on mounting hopes for US-Iran de-escalation
headlines.summary: Crude prices dropped as traders gained confidence that renewed conflict between the US and Iran was becoming less likely, with peace negotiations showing signs of progress.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Rise on US-Iran Deal Uncertainty and Oman Terminal Suspension
perspectives.summary: Oil prices rebounded after Hezbollah rejected a Lebanon ceasefire proposal and Oman's Mina al Fahal terminal suspended oil loadings following an explosion, raising supply concerns amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.

headlines.headline: US forces board sanctioned tanker in Indian Ocean
headlines.summary: The US military conducted an interdiction of a stateless oil tanker in the Indian Ocean overnight, continuing enforcement of sanctions amid heightened regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: US Forces Board Sanctioned Tanker in Indian Ocean
perspectives.summary: U.S. forces conducted an interdiction of the stateless oil tanker Davina in the Indian Ocean, enforcing sanctions amid ongoing tensions with Iran over maritime security.

headlines.headline: Trump vows quick end to Iran war in Wisconsin campaign stop
headlines.summary: President Trump promised to swiftly end the Iran conflict and remove a source of high prices while campaigning for Republicans in rural Wisconsin ahead of midterm elections.

perspectives.angle: US House Votes to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
perspectives.summary: The House passed a war powers resolution 215-208, with four Republicans joining Democrats in a rare rebuke of President Trump's handling of the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: SpaceX locks in Google cloud deal ahead of IPO
headlines.summary: SpaceX signed a multi-year cloud services agreement with Google for computing capacity, securing additional revenue streams ahead of its stock market debut next week.

headlines.headline: US Approves $1.98 Billion Counter-Drone System Sale to Kuwait
headlines.summary: The US State Department approved a major sale of counter-unmanned aerial systems to Kuwait as Gulf states ramp up air defenses amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UN Security Council Demands Release of Houthi-Detained Staff After Two Years
headlines.summary: Council members renewed calls for the release of UN and NGO personnel held by Yemen Houthi rebels for two years, warning that detentions worsen humanitarian conditions.

headlines.headline: Most Asia-Pacific Firms Using AI Without Job Cuts, Survey Finds
headlines.summary: About 74% of companies surveyed across the Asia-Pacific region have deployed or piloted AI programs while maintaining workforce levels.

headlines.headline: South Korean Defense Stocks Surge on Middle East Demand
headlines.summary: Defense stocks in South Korea are climbing as Middle Eastern countries seek faster deliveries of Korean-made missile defense systems.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy calls for direct negotiations with Putin in neutral country
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy wrote a public letter to Putin proposing face-to-face talks hosted by a neutral third country, suggesting Switzerland, Turkey or Arab states. He proposed a full ceasefire during negotiations and an all-for-all prisoner exchange as a first step.

headlines.headline: Senate passes  billion immigration enforcement bill after all-night session
headlines.summary: The Senate approved funding for ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump's term after Republicans defeated multiple attempts to ban Trump's controversial .776 billion settlement fund for allies claiming political persecution.

headlines.headline: Half of Latin Americans deported from US to Congo have returned home
headlines.summary: More than half of the 15 Latin Americans deported to Congo under Trump's third-country deportation policy have returned to their countries of origin, even though US judges ruled they likely face persecution at home.

headlines.headline: US House Defies Trump, Passes Ukraine Aid Package and Russia Sanctions
headlines.summary: The US House approved legislation supporting Ukraine with direct loans and assistance while imposing new sanctions on Russia, marking a significant rebuke of President Trump's foreign policy stance.

perspectives.angle: US House Passes Russia Sanctions and Ukraine Aid Bill
perspectives.summary: The House voted 226-195 for the Ukraine Support Act, with Republicans joining Democrats to force the vote. The bill had languished for months before reaching the floor.

headlines.headline: China Tracks Dutch Frigate Through Taiwan Strait
headlines.summary: China's military dispatched naval and air assets to monitor the Dutch frigate De Ruyter during its transit through the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions in the region.

perspectives.angle: Chinese Military Tracks Dutch Frigate Transiting Taiwan Strait
perspectives.summary: Chinas military dispatched naval and air assets to monitor a Dutch warship passing through the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions over international waterways in the region.

headlines.headline: Irish PM Bans Israeli Ministers Over Flotilla Comments
headlines.summary: Ireland banned Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich and National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir for anti-Palestinian remarks and actions related to the Gaza flotilla, calling their behavior a push for the "elimination of Palestinians from Palestine."

headlines.headline: Slovenia Removes Palestinian Flag in Sharp Pro-Israel Shift
headlines.summary: Slovenia's new prime minister removed the Palestinian flag from government buildings in a symbolic break from the previous administration's stance, following Slovenia's earlier condemnation of Israel over Gaza.

headlines.headline: Russian Budget Deficit Doubles to $81 Billion Amid War Strain
headlines.summary: Russia's federal budget deficit reached 6 trillion rubles ($81.4 billion) in the first five months of 2026, doubling from the same period last year as war costs continue to mount.

headlines.headline: ISS Astronauts Briefly Ordered to Prepare for Evacuation
headlines.summary: NASA ordered five astronauts to shelter in docked SpaceX Dragon spacecraft for two hours as a Russian crew attempted to fix a worsening air leak in the Zvezda service module. Operations have since returned to normal.

perspectives.angle: ISS astronauts briefly take shelter during leak repair on Russian side
perspectives.summary: NASA ordered astronauts to shelter in a SpaceX capsule while Russian cosmonauts worked to fix a fresh leak aboard the International Space Station. The crew returned to normal operations after repairs were paused.

headlines.headline: SpaceX IPO Bars China and Hong Kong Investors
headlines.summary: SpaceX underwriters have barred investors in China and Hong Kong from participating in the IPO, with the company seeking to raise $75 billion in what could be one of the largest public offerings ever.

headlines.headline: US House Approves Ukraine Aid and Russia Sanctions
headlines.summary: The House passed the Ukraine Support Act 226-195 after a discharge petition forced the vote, defying President Trump and Senate Republicans. A handful of Republicans joined Democrats in supporting the legislation.

headlines.headline: Somalia Government Restores Order After Capital Fighting
headlines.summary: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and militias allied with opposition politicians clashed, forcing some civilians to flee.

perspectives.angle: Somalia Restores Order After Capital Fighting Between Government and Opposition Militias
perspectives.summary: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and opposition-allied militias exchanged fire, forcing civilians to flee the capital.

headlines.headline: Russia and Uzbekistan Begin Construction of First Nuclear Power Plant in Central Asia
headlines.summary: The Kremlin announced the start of construction on a Russian-built nuclear power plant in Uzbekistan, the first in post-Soviet Central Asia, aimed at meeting growing regional energy demands.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Destroys Russian Drone Base at Occupied Donetsk Airport
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces conducted an operation to degrade Russia's military capabilities at the occupied Donetsk Airport, destroying a drone base. The operation continues Ukraine's strategy of targeting Russian military infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine releases footage of drones striking Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base near St. Petersburg
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces published video of drones attacking a Russian warship armed with guided missiles at the Kronstadt naval base, demonstrating Kyiv's reach into Russia's Baltic waters.

headlines.headline: Armenia Faces Pivotal Election on Future Away From Russia
headlines.summary: Armenia's parliamentary election on June 7 will test whether the small democracy can chart its own future and how much the country will pivot away from Russian influence toward the West.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Volcano Eruption Forces Airport Closure
headlines.summary: Mount Lewotobi Laki-Laki in Indonesia erupted, forcing a local airport to close. The volcano is under Indonesia's second-highest alert level with a 5km exclusion zone.

headlines.headline: Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Be Handed to Ukraine
headlines.summary: A Swedish court ruled that a cargo vessel seized in the Baltic Sea can be handed over to Ukraine. The ship is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

perspectives.angle: Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Go to Ukraine
perspectives.summary: A Swedish court ruled that a cargo vessel seized in the Baltic Sea can be handed to Ukraine. The ship is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

headlines.headline: US Judge Strikes Down Trump Immigration Policy on 39 Countries
headlines.summary: A federal judge invalidated Trump administration policies that made it harder for immigrants from dozens of countries to receive decisions, ruling the measures unlawful after the shooting of two National Guard members.

perspectives.angle: US federal judge strikes down Trump immigration policy affecting 39 countries
perspectives.summary: A federal judge in Boston struck down a Trump administration policy that made it harder for immigrants from 39 African, Asian, Latin American and Middle Eastern countries to stay in the US. The ruling said the policy threw immigrants into "indeterminate legal limbo."

headlines.headline: Russian Satellites Jamming GPS Across Europe
